name: All purpose cleaner
ratio: 50 ml white vinegar, 30 g baking soda, 50 ml soap
steps: Pour the white vinegar into the pot, add the baking soda, stir until dissolved, then add the soap, stir evenly, and it is ready to use.

name: glass cleaner
ratio: 200 ml alcohol, 100 ml white vinegar, 100 ml water
steps: Pour alcohol, white vinegar, and water into a container, stir evenly, and put it into a sprayer to use.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: kitchen grease cleaner
ratio: 30g baking soda, 20ml dish soap, 500ml warm water
steps: Dissolve baking soda in warm water, add dish soap and mix well. Spray on the oily surface, let it sit for 5 minutes and then wipe with a rag.

name: Bathroom descaler
ratio: 20g citric acid, 15g baking soda, 300ml water
steps: Dissolve citric acid and baking soda in water, stir until completely dissolved, and pour into a spray bottle. Spray on the scale and let it sit for 10 minutes, then scrub, and finally rinse with water.
safetyNote: It is recommended to wear gloves when operating and avoid prolonged contact with skin.

name: furniture maintenance wax
ratio: 30 g beeswax, 100 ml olive oil, 20 ml turpentine
steps: Heat beeswax over water until completely melted, add olive oil and turpentine, stir evenly. After it cools down slightly, apply a small amount and wipe the surface of the furniture, then polish with a dry cloth.
safetyNote: Turpentine is flammable and has a pungent smell. Please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: carpet stain remover
ratio: 100 ml white vinegar, 10 ml dish soap, 400 ml warm water
steps: Mix white vinegar, dish soap and warm water and stir until foamy. Use a soft-bristled brush dipped in foam to gently brush the stained area, then use a clean damp cloth to absorb excess water.

name: silverware polish
ratio: 30g baking soda, 15g salt, 1 piece of aluminum foil, 500ml hot water
steps: Place crumpled aluminum foil on the bottom of the container, add baking soda and salt, pour in hot water and stir to dissolve. Soak the silverware for 5 to 10 minutes, then rinse and dry it to restore its luster.
safetyNote: Be careful to prevent scalding when operating in hot water. Silverware should not be soaked for too long to avoid damaging the carvings.

name: Air conditioning filter cleaner
ratio: 15 ml dish soap, 50 ml white vinegar, 1000 ml warm water
steps: Add dish soap and white vinegar to warm water and mix well. Remove the filter and soak it for 15 minutes. Brush lightly with a soft brush, rinse with water, dry in the shade and reinstall it.
safetyNote: Be sure to disconnect the power supply of the air conditioner before cleaning, and do not put it back in before the filter is completely dry.

name: Tile gap cleaner
ratio: 100 ml hydrogen peroxide, 30 g baking soda
steps: Make a paste of baking soda and hydrogen peroxide and use an old toothbrush to scrub the gaps between the tiles. Let it sit for 10 minutes, wipe it with a damp cloth, and finally wipe the surface with clean water.
safetyNote: Hydrogen peroxide has bleaching properties. Please try applying in an inconspicuous area first to avoid contact with the surface of colored tiles.

name: stainless steel cleaner
ratio: 30g flour, 50ml white vinegar, 200ml warm water
steps: Add warm water to flour to make a paste, add white vinegar and stir evenly. Use a soft cloth to wipe the stainless steel surface, polish along the grain direction, and finally wipe it dry with clean water.

name: microwave deodorizer
ratio: 1 lemon, 200 ml water
steps: Place lemon slices in a heatproof bowl and add water. Place it in the microwave and heat it on high for 3 minutes, let it sit for 5 minutes, then take it out and wipe the inner cavity with a damp cloth to remove the smell.
safetyNote: The bowl will be very hot after heating, please use heat-resistant gloves when taking it out.

name: hot water kettle descaler
ratio: 30 g citric acid, 500 ml water
steps: Dissolve citric acid in water, pour it into a kettle, heat to a slight boil, then turn off the power and soak for 30 minutes. Pour away the solution and rinse twice to remove scale.
safetyNote: Do not close the lid tightly when descaling to avoid excessive steam pressure. Not for use with aluminum kettles.

name: bathroom mold remover
ratio: 50 ml bleach, 450 ml water
steps: Dilute bleach with water and put it into a spray bottle and spray it on moldy walls or cracks. Let it sit for 15 minutes, then brush, rinse with water and keep ventilated and dry.
safetyNote: Bleach is corrosive and irritating. Please wear gloves and keep it ventilated. Do not mix it with acidic cleaners.

name: oven cleaner
ratio: 50g baking soda, 15ml dish soap, 100ml warm water
steps: Mix baking soda with a small amount of warm water to make a thick paste, and apply it to the oily areas on the inner wall of the oven. After leaving it overnight, wipe it off with a damp cloth, wash it with dish soap, and finally dry it.
safetyNote: Be sure to turn off the power and cool down before cleaning to avoid the cleaning agent coming into contact with the heating tube.

name: faucet luminous agent
ratio: 15 grams of toothpaste, 10 grams of baking soda
steps: Mix toothpaste and baking soda to form a paste, dip it in a soft cloth and wipe the surface of the faucet. Polish along the arc direction, rinse with clean water and polish with a dry cloth.

name: leather sofa cleaner
ratio: 30 ml olive oil, 20 ml white vinegar, 100 ml water
steps: Mix the three ingredients evenly, then dip a soft cloth into it and gently wipe the leather surface. After wiping, use a clean damp cloth to wipe off the residue. After drying naturally, the leather will regain its luster.
safetyNote: Before use, test it on an inconspicuous area to avoid discoloration.

name: shoe deodorizing spray
ratio: 80 ml alcohol, 3 ml tea tree essential oil, 20 ml water
steps: Mix the alcohol and essential oils with water, shake well, and put into a spray bottle. Spray the inside 2 to 3 times after each wear, and dry in the shade to deodorize and sterilize.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, keep away from fire sources. Please try spraying new shoes or valuable materials first.

name: screen cleaner
ratio: 10 ml dish soap, 30 ml white vinegar, 500 ml warm water
steps: Dissolve dish soap and white vinegar in warm water and use a soft-bristled brush to scrub both sides of the screen. Rinse with clean water after brushing, dry and reinstall.

name: brassware polish
ratio: 30 ml lemon juice, 10 g salt, 20 g flour
steps: Mix salt with flour and add lemon juice to make a paste. Apply to the brass surface and let it sit for 10 minutes. Wipe it with a soft cloth, rinse with water and dry.

name: Machine lubricants
ratio: 200 ml gasoline, 100 ml liquid soap
steps: Pour the gasoline into the container, add the soap solution, stir evenly and use.
safetyNote: Gasoline is highly flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area, away from open flames and children.

name: Bicycle chain lubricant
ratio: Liquid paraffin 50 g, gasoline 50 ml
steps: Mix liquid paraffin and gasoline, stir well and use.
safetyNote: Gasoline is highly flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area, away from open flames and children.

name: Door and window hinge lubricant
ratio: 20 grams of petroleum jelly, 30 ml of engine oil
steps: Place Vaseline and engine oil in a temperature-resistant container and heat over water until completely combined. After cooling, dip a cotton swab in it and apply it to the hinge shaft.
safetyNote: Used engine oil may contain impurities and is recommended for lubrication of non-precision parts only.

name: leather care oil
ratio: 50g petroleum jelly, 20g lanolin, 2ml essence
steps: Heat the vaseline and lanolin over water to melt, then remove from the heat and add the essence and stir evenly. After cooling, apply a small amount to the leather surface and rub evenly with a soft cloth.

name: sewing machine lubricant
ratio: 80 ml white oil, 20 ml alcohol
steps: Pour the white oil into a clean container, add alcohol and stir evenly. Use a dropper to take a small amount and drop it into the moving parts of the sewing machine, and run it for several turns to distribute the oil evenly.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: clock lubricant
ratio: White oil 90 ml, spermaceti 5 g
steps: Chop the spermaceti into pieces and add to the white oil. Stir over low heat over water until completely dissolved. After cooling, use a very fine needle to take a small amount and apply it on the bearings of the watch. Do not overdo it.
safetyNote: It is recommended that precision watches be maintained by professionals. Self-lubrication may cause abnormal timing.

name: Wire rope grease
ratio: Grease 50 g, kerosene 30 ml
steps: Place the grease in a container and gradually add kerosene and stir to dilute to a paintable consistency. Apply evenly on the surface of the wire rope and run it several times to allow the grease to penetrate into the strands.
safetyNote: Kerosene is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources. Please recycle waste oil properly.

name: Wooden drawer slide oil
ratio: 15 g beeswax, 50 ml flax oil
steps: Melt the beeswax over water, add linseed oil and stir evenly. After cooling, apply a small amount to the wooden drawer slides and track grooves, and push and pull several times.

name: Fan bearing lubricant
ratio: Lubricating oil 40 ml, kerosene 10 ml
steps: Mix the lubricating oil and kerosene evenly, use a dropper to take 1 to 2 drops and inject them into the fan bearing. Manually turn the fan blades several times to distribute the oil, wipe off excess oil stains, and then turn on the power for a test run.
safetyNote: Be sure to disconnect the power supply before operation. Kerosene is flammable and should be kept away from fire sources.

name: Iron anti-rust oil
ratio: 80 ml engine oil, 20 ml wax oil
steps: Mix the engine oil and wax oil and stir evenly. Use a brush to apply it thinly to a clean, dry iron surface, and let it dry to form a protective film, which can slow down rust.
safetyNote: Waste engine oil may contain harmful substances. Please wear gloves when handling and avoid contact with skin.

name: Mold release oil
ratio: 100 ml cooking oil, 10 g beeswax
steps: Heat the beeswax over water and melt, add cooking oil and stir evenly. After cooling, apply it to the inner wall of the mold with a brush to facilitate the demoulding of concrete or gypsum products.

name: zipper lubrication wax
ratio: 10 grams of candles, 15 grams of Vaseline
steps: Melt the chopped candles and Vaseline over water and mix well. After cooling, use a cotton swab to dip it in, apply it between the zipper teeth, and pull it several times until it becomes smooth.

name: Instrument peg lubricant
ratio: Graphite powder 2 g, vegetable oil 20 ml
steps: Add graphite powder to vegetable oil and stir evenly. Dip a toothpick into a small amount and apply it to the thread of the peg, twist it in and out several times to evenly distribute the oil film.
safetyNote: The dosage should be very small to prevent oil from penetrating into the soundboard and affecting the tone.

name: Skateboard wheel bearing oil
ratio: Lubricant 15 ml, detergent 5 ml
steps: After removing the bearing and cleaning it from dust, add 1 to 2 drops of lubricating oil. Manually rotate the bearing to allow oil to seep in, wipe off excess grease on the surface and then reinstall it.
safetyNote: The cleaning agent is flammable, keep away from fire sources when operating.

name: Anti-rust oil for garden tools
ratio: Waste oil 100 ml, diesel 30 ml
steps: Mix waste engine oil and diesel evenly, and use a brush to apply it to the metal cutting edges and joints of tools such as shovels and shears. Storing after drying can slow down rust.
safetyNote: Waste engine oil contains harmful substances, please wear gloves and store it properly to avoid children's contact.

name: Printer rail lubricant
ratio: Silicone oil 20 ml
steps: After the power is turned off, clean the dust on the guide rails. Use a cotton swab to dip a small amount of silicone oil and apply it evenly on the surface of the slide rails. Manually push the print head back and forth several times, wipe off excess silicone oil, and then power on to test the machine.
safetyNote: Do not use too much to avoid silicone oil contaminating printing components.

name: fishing reel grease
ratio: 20 grams of grease, 10 grams of petroleum jelly
steps: Mix grease and Vaseline and knead evenly, then fill a small amount into the gear mesh of the fishing reel. Turn the wheel several times to distribute the grease and wipe off any excess.

name: Lock cylinder lubricant
ratio: Graphite powder 3 grams, engine oil 10 ml
steps: Mix graphite powder and engine oil into a thin paste, and drop it into the lock cylinder hole with a straw. Repeatedly insert and remove the key to rotate the lock cylinder so that the lubricant evenly covers the pin mechanism.
safetyNote: It is not advisable to use excessive liquid oil to avoid adsorbing dust and causing blockage of the lock core.

name: watercolor paint
ratio: 50g gelatin, 10g pigment, 400ml water
steps: Add gelatin powder to water, heat until gelatin dissolves, add pigment, stir evenly and use.
safetyNote: Please be careful of burns when heating. It is recommended to operate under adult supervision.

name: oil paint
ratio: 100 ml linseed oil, 20 g pigment, 5 g rosin, 5 ml dry tempering agent
steps: Pour the linseed oil into the container, add the pigment and rosin, stir evenly, then add the dry seasoning agent, stir evenly and use.

name: pastel paint
ratio: 30 grams of gypsum powder, 5 grams of gum arabic, appropriate amount of pigment
steps: Dissolve gum arabic in a small amount of warm water, add gypsum powder and pigment, grind it repeatedly with a grinding board until it becomes a fine paste, put it into a palette and let it dry.
safetyNote: To avoid inhaling dust when grinding, it is recommended to wear a mask.

name: Lime mural paint
ratio: 200 grams of lime mortar, 10 grams of pigment, 5 grams of hemp knife
steps: Add the pigment to the lime slurry and mix thoroughly until the color is uniform, then add the hemp fiber and mix well. You can use a brush to apply it on the wall or mural manuscript.
safetyNote: Lime is alkaline, so wear gloves and goggles when handling it.

name: acrylic paint
ratio: Acrylic emulsion 100 ml, paint 15 g, water 30 ml
steps: Gradually add the paint to the acrylic emulsion and stir evenly with a palette knife. Depending on the consistency, add an appropriate amount of water to adjust until it is suitable for brushing.

name: tempera paint
ratio: 1 egg yolk, 5 grams of pigment, 10 ml of water
steps: Remove the egg white from the egg yolk and add a small amount of water to form an emulsion. Gradually add the pigment and grind until fine and even, then it can be used for tempera painting on wood panels or murals.
safetyNote: Mineral pigments should be used as pigments. They should be mixed and used immediately and should not be stored for a long time.

name: homemade ink
ratio: 10 grams of carbon black, 5 grams of gum arabic, 5 ml of glycerin, 100 ml of water
steps: Dissolve gum arabic in warm water, add carbon black and grind repeatedly until fine and particle-free, then add glycerin and stir evenly. After filtering, put it into an ink bottle and let it stand for 1 day before use.
safetyNote: To avoid inhaling dust when grinding carbon black, it is recommended to wear a mask.

name: colored chalk
ratio: 100 grams of gypsum powder, 5 grams of pigment, 40 ml of water
steps: Dry mix the gypsum powder and pigment evenly, gradually add water and knead into a ball, fill it into a chalk mold and compact it. Take it out and dry it in the shade for 2 to 3 days. It can be used after it is completely hardened.
safetyNote: Avoid inhaling dust when drying the gypsum powder, and tap lightly when demoulding the mold to avoid cracking.

name: wall latex paint
ratio: 200 grams of lime, 10 grams of pigment, 50 ml of white latex, 300 ml of water
steps: Add lime to water to make a paste, add pigment and stir evenly, then mix in white latex to adjust adhesion. After adjusting to a paintable consistency, use a roller or brush to paint the wall.
safetyNote: Lime is alkaline, so please wear gloves and a mask when handling it.

name: Metal anti-rust paint
ratio: 100 ml alkyd resin, 15 g pigment, 30 ml turpentine
steps: Add the pigment to the alkyd resin, grind and stir until uniform, then add turpentine to adjust the consistency. Apply to the rust-removed metal surface and allow to dry naturally for 24 hours before applying twice.
safetyNote: Turpentine is flammable and irritating. Please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: fabric dye solution
ratio: 10 grams of dye, 30 grams of salt, 20 ml of white vinegar, 1000 ml of water
steps: Add salt and dye to water, heat to 60°C, stir to dissolve, and add white vinegar. After the fabric is soaked, put it into the dyeing solution and stir for 30 minutes for dyeing. Rinse with water until it is clear and dry.
safetyNote: Be careful to avoid scalding when heating the dye solution. The dye may stain the skin, so it is recommended to wear gloves.

name: mural base paint
ratio: 100 g lithopone, 30 ml glue, 200 ml water
steps: Dissolve the glue in water, gradually add lithopone and stir until a uniform paste forms. Use a scraper to apply a thin layer of paint on the wall as a mural primer, then paint it after it dries.
safetyNote: To avoid inhaling dust when mixing, it is recommended to wear a mask.

name: Asphalt paint
ratio: 50 grams of asphalt, 30 ml of turpentine, 10 grams of pigment
steps: Heat the asphalt over water until it becomes fluid, add turpentine and pigment and stir evenly. Adjust to a consistency suitable for scraping and can be used on wood or canvas.
safetyNote: Asphalt and turpentine are irritating and flammable. Please operate in a ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: spray paint thinner
ratio: 100 ml acrylic paint, 20 ml thinner, 10 ml water
steps: Add thinner to acrylic paint and stir evenly. Add appropriate amount of water to adjust according to spray consistency. After filtering, load it into a spray gun or spray can and spray thinly several times to obtain an even coating.
safetyNote: Thinner is flammable and toxic, so work must be done in a well-ventilated area and wear a protective mask.

name: blackboard paint
ratio: 200 grams of cement paint, 5 grams of pigment, 50 ml of water
steps: Add water to cement paint to a paintable consistency, add black pigment and stir evenly. Apply twice on a flat surface and write with chalk after it dries.

name: wood stain
ratio: 50g walnut peel, 100ml vinegar, 300ml water
steps: Boil walnut skins in water for 30 minutes, filter and add vinegar. Apply to the polished wood surface while it's hot or cool to create a natural tan.
safetyNote: Be careful of burns when cooking. The coloring depth can be adjusted by the number of times you apply it.

name: Enamel pigments
ratio: 20g borax, 30g quartz powder, 8g pigment
steps: Dry mix borax and quartz powder evenly, add pigment and grind until fine. Add a small amount of water to prepare the glaze, apply it to the metal body and then fire it at high temperature to develop the color.
safetyNote: Firing needs to be done in a professional kiln, and protective equipment must be worn during operation.

name: stage makeup paint
ratio: 30 grams of corn starch, appropriate amount of food coloring, 10 ml of glycerin
steps: Mix cornstarch and glycerin into a paste, and gradually add food coloring to adjust to the desired color. Can be applied directly to skin and removed with warm water.
safetyNote: For external use only, please do a skin test first if you have sensitive skin.

name: household glue
ratio: 20 g gelatin, 10 g glycerin, 2 ml acetic acid
steps: Add gelatin to hot water, heat until gelatin is completely dissolved, add glycerin and acetic acid, stir evenly and use.
safetyNote: Acetic acid is irritating, please maintain ventilation during operation and avoid contact with eyes.

name: woodworking glue
ratio: 20g gelatin, 10g protein powder, 50ml hot water
steps: Add gelatin powder to hot water, heat until gelatin dissolves, add protein powder, stir evenly and use.
safetyNote: Please be careful of burns when heating. It is recommended to operate under adult supervision.

name: flour paste
ratio: 50 g flour, 200 ml water
steps: Add flour to water to make a uniform paste, heat over low heat and stir continuously, cook until transparent and sticky. After cooling from the fire, put it into an airtight container. Paper and lightweight materials can be pasted on it.
safetyNote: When heating, be careful not to burn the bottom and avoid burns.

name: rubber glue
ratio: 10 grams of raw rubber, 50 ml of gasoline, 5 grams of rosin
steps: Cut the raw rubber into small pieces, soak it in gasoline until it expands and softens, add rosin over water and heat and stir until completely dissolved. After cooling, it can be used to repair rubber products.
safetyNote: Gasoline is highly flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area, away from open flames and children.

name: white latex
ratio: Polyvinyl acetate emulsion 200 ml, talc 10 g, carboxymethylcellulose 2 g
steps: Dissolve carboxymethyl cellulose in a small amount of warm water to make a glue solution, add talc powder and stir evenly, then mix it with polyvinyl acetate emulsion and adjust it to an appropriate consistency before use.

name: bone glue
ratio: 30 g bone glue particles, 150 ml water
steps: Soak the bone gel particles in cold water for 2 hours until they swell, heat and stir over water until completely dissolved and become transparent. Used to join wood while hot, forming a strong bond once cooled.
safetyNote: Please be careful to avoid burns when heating. The glue should be dissolved and used immediately. It will solidify again after cooling.

name: Foam glue
ratio: Polyvinyl acetate emulsion 150 ml, foam chips 20 g
steps: Cut the foam into fine particles, add it to the polyvinyl acetate emulsion and stir thoroughly until it becomes a uniform paste. Apply to the adhesive surface of foam or fabric and fix with pressure until dry.
safetyNote: Maintain ventilation when bonding, and do not move the adherend before it is completely dry.

name: Strong adhesive tape base
ratio: 15 grams of rosin, 8 grams of raw rubber, 60 ml of gasoline
steps: Cut raw rubber into pieces and soak in gasoline, add rosin and heat over water and stir until completely dissolved. Apply it thinly to the tape base material while it is hot. It will have strong adhesion after cooling.
safetyNote: Gasoline is highly flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area, away from open flames and children.

name: Epoxy resin glue
ratio: 100 grams of epoxy resin, 30 grams of curing agent
steps: Mix the epoxy resin and curing agent according to the weight ratio, and stir thoroughly for 2 to 3 minutes until the color is uniform. Apply bonding within working time and allow 24 hours to cure completely.
safetyNote: The reaction is exothermic after mixing, so avoid mixing in large quantities. Wear gloves and maintain ventilation when operating.

name: stamp glue
ratio: 15 grams of gum arabic, 5 ml of glycerin, 100 ml of water
steps: Dissolve gum arabic in warm water, add glycerin, stir evenly, filter and put into a vial. Apply it to the envelope seal or the back of the stamp. After drying, it will stick firmly and be easy to remove when wet.

name: starch adhesive
ratio: 50g potato starch, 400ml water, 2g boric acid
steps: Add water to starch to make a suspension, heat over low heat and stir continuously until transparent and viscous. Add boric acid to prevent corrosion. After cooling, it can be used for bonding paper and cartons.
safetyNote: Boric acid is not edible, please label it and store it out of the reach of children.

name: book binding glue
ratio: 25 grams of animal glue, 8 grams of glycerin, 120 ml of water
steps: After soaking the animal glue, heat it over water to dissolve, add glycerin and stir evenly. Apply to the spine cloth and paper folds while hot, bind under pressure, and bond firmly after cooling.
safetyNote: Be careful of burns when heating. The glue should be used freshly.

name: Tile adhesive
ratio: 200 grams of cement, 400 grams of sand, 50 ml of latex
steps: Dry mix cement and sand evenly, add latex and appropriate amount of water and mix until thick. After applying to the wall, lay the tiles and tap them firmly with a rubber hammer.
safetyNote: Cement is alkaline, so please wear gloves and a mask when handling it.

name: Natural paint glue
ratio: 50 ml raw lacquer, 10 g rosin
steps: Heat and melt the rosin over water. When the temperature drops to about 60°C, add raw lacquer and stir thoroughly until uniform. Can be used for wood splicing and paint sealing.
safetyNote: Raw paint may cause skin irritation, so be sure to wear gloves and operate in a ventilated area.

name: hot melt glue stick
ratio: Ethylene vinyl acetate 80 g, paraffin 20 g
steps: Heat the two raw materials over water until completely melted, stir evenly and pour into a rod-shaped mold. After cooling and hardening, it can be loaded into a hot melt glue gun and heated for use.
safetyNote: The heating temperature is high, so be careful to prevent scalding and keep away from children.

name: waterproof sealant
ratio: 100 grams of asphalt, 30 ml of kerosene, 20 grams of talcum powder
steps: Heat and melt the asphalt, add kerosene to dilute it, and mix in talcum powder to adjust to a suitable consistency. Fill in gaps or apply to joints while it's hot, and it will become waterproof and sealed after cooling.
safetyNote: Asphalt and kerosene are flammable, please operate in a ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: wallpaper glue
ratio: 40g potato starch, 500ml water, 3g alum
steps: Mix the starch with water and heat over low heat until it becomes a transparent paste. Add the alum solution and stir evenly. After cooling, use a roller to apply to the back and wall of the wallpaper.
safetyNote: Keep stirring while heating to prevent the mixture from burning.

name: ceramic repair glue
ratio: 20 grams of porcelain powder, 30 grams of epoxy resin, 9 grams of curing agent
steps: Mix the epoxy resin with the curing agent, add the porcelain powder and stir until it has a toothpaste-like consistency. After filling the chip in the porcelain, set it for 24 hours and then sand it smooth when dry.
safetyNote: After mixing, please use it within the operating time and wear gloves when operating.

name: perfume
ratio: 100 ml alcohol, 5 g essential oil, 50 ml water
steps: Pour the alcohol and water into the container, add the essential oil, stir evenly, and leave it for a period of time to create the perfume.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: solid balm
ratio: 20g beeswax, 50g coconut oil, 3ml essential oil
steps: Heat the beeswax and coconut oil over water until they are completely melted. Remove from the heat and wait until the temperature drops slightly. Add the essential oil and stir evenly. Pour it into a small box to cool and set before use.

name: toilet water
ratio: Alcohol 150 ml, menthol 3 g, essence 2 ml, water 50 ml
steps: Dissolve menthol in alcohol, add flavor and water, shake well and let it sit overnight, then put it into a spray bottle for use.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources. Avoid contact with eyes and wounds.

name: wardrobe sachet
ratio: 30 grams of dried lavender, 20 grams of dried bay leaves, 2 ml of essential oil
steps: Mix the dried lavender and dried bay leaves evenly, add drops of essential oil and mix well, put it into a breathable cloth bag, tie the bag tightly, and hang it or place it in the wardrobe.

name: room aromatherapy spray
ratio: 80 ml water, 20 ml alcohol, 5 ml essential oil
steps: Mix the alcohol and essential oils and shake well, then add water, mix well, and pour into a spray bottle. Shake well before use and spray into the air.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: car aromatherapy tablets
ratio: 50 g diatomaceous earth, 5 ml essential oil, 10 ml water
steps: Diatomaceous earth is mixed with water and essential oils, kneaded well, and pressed into molds to form sheets or blocks. Dry in the shade for 1 to 2 days and then place it in a car aromatherapy holder to slowly release the aroma with the temperature.
safetyNote: Do not place aromatherapy tablets in direct sunlight to avoid high temperatures causing essential oils to evaporate too quickly.

name: bath salts
ratio: 200g sea salt, 50g baking soda, 5ml essential oil
steps: Mix sea salt and baking soda evenly, drop in essential oil, mix thoroughly, and put into an airtight jar. Dissolve 30 grams in hot water in the bathtub for each bath.
safetyNote: If you have skin wounds or allergies, please try a small amount first to avoid direct contact of essential oils with mucous membranes.

name: sandalwood incense
ratio: 80 grams of sandalwood powder, 20 grams of nanmu powder, 30 ml of water
steps: Mix sandalwood powder and nanmu powder evenly, gradually add water and knead into a ball, and squeeze it into shape with an incense stick mold. Dry in the shade for 3 to 5 days until it is completely dry, then it can be ignited and used.
safetyNote: When burning, please place it in a fire-proof incense stick, keep the room ventilated, and keep people away from the fire source.

name: Wormwood sachet
ratio: 40 grams of dried mugwort leaves, 10 grams of cloves, 1 cotton bag
steps: Mix dried mugwort leaves and cloves evenly, put them into a cotton bag and tie the bag tightly. Hang it indoors or place it next to a pillow to emit herbal fragrance.

name: Osmanthus sachet
ratio: 25g dried osmanthus, 50g brown rice, 2ml essential oil
steps: Mix brown rice and dried osmanthus, drop in essential oil, mix well, put into gauze bags and tie tightly. Place it in a closet or drawer and it will continue to emit the scent of osmanthus.

name: Incense stick
ratio: 10g agarwood powder, 20g sandalwood powder, 40g coconut oil, 15g beeswax
steps: Heat beeswax and coconut oil over water to melt. Remove from heat, add agarwood powder and sandalwood powder and stir evenly. Pour into a container to cool and set, then take a small amount and heat it in an incense burner to release the aroma.
safetyNote: Maintain ventilation when heating incense and avoid prolonged use in a confined space.

name: Dried flower scent bag
ratio: 20g dried rose, 20g dried lavender, 2ml essential oil
steps: Mix the dried flowers evenly, drop in the essential oil and mix well, then put it into a gauze bag and tie it tightly. Place it on your bedside or in your closet to continuously distribute floral fragrance.

name: Mugwort mosquito repellent incense
ratio: 60g mugwort powder, 20g mint powder, 20g sticky powder
steps: Mix the three powders evenly, add appropriate amount of water and knead into a ball, then squeeze into a cone or string shape. Dry in the shade for 3 days and then ignite to release a mosquito-repellent aroma.
safetyNote: When burning, place it in a fireproof container, keep it ventilated, and keep people away from the fire source.

name: jasmine fragrance
ratio: 50g fresh jasmine, 100ml alcohol, 10ml glycerin
steps: Jasmine flowers are soaked in alcohol and sealed for 7 days. After filtering, glycerin is added and shaken well. Put it in a spray bottle and use it as facial or clothing fragrance.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, keep away from fire sources. If you have sensitive skin, please do a skin test first.

name: Natural mosquito repellent essential oil
ratio: Lemongrass essential oil 5 ml, Eucalyptus essential oil 3 ml, carrier oil 50 ml
steps: Mix the three oils, shake well, and put into a roll-on or spray bottle. Apply to exposed areas such as wrists and ankles to help repel mosquitoes.
safetyNote: Avoid contact with eyes and mucous membranes, use with caution on pregnant women and infants.

name: sandalwood incense powder
ratio: 50 grams of sandalwood powder, 15 grams of agarwood powder, 35 grams of nanmu sticky powder
steps: Mix the three powders evenly, add appropriate amounts of water and knead into a dough, then knead into tower incense or powder. After drying in the shade, you can place it in an incense burner and light it for use.
safetyNote: Please maintain ventilation when burning and use fire-proof incense.

name: antiperspirant deodorant spray
ratio: Alcohol 80 ml, aloe vera juice 20 ml, essential oil 3 ml
steps: Mix alcohol with aloe vera juice, add essential oils, shake, and let sit for 24 hours. Pour into a spray bottle and spray on armpits or areas of the body prone to sweating.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable. Do not use if there is a wound on the skin.

name: laundry detergent
ratio: 50 grams of baking soda, 50 ml of liquid soap, appropriate amount of spices
steps: Mix baking soda and liquid soap, add spices, stir evenly to make laundry detergent.

name: Multipurpose laundry detergent
ratio: 100g baking soda, 50g borax, 50g soap powder
steps: Pour the baking soda, borax and soap powder into a dry container and stir well. Use 20 to 30 grams for each wash. Washing with warm water will give better results.
safetyNote: Borax is not edible and should be kept out of the reach of children.

name: Hand detergent
ratio: Coconut oil soap 50g, glycerin 10ml, warm water 300ml
steps: Chop coconut oil soap and dissolve in warm water, stir until completely dissolved, add glycerin and continue stirring. After cooling to room temperature, put it into a squeeze bottle and use it.

name: fabric softener
ratio: 100 ml white vinegar, 400 ml water, 2 ml essential oil
steps: Mix white vinegar and water, drop in essential oil, mix well, and put into a bottle. Add 50 ml during the last rinse, soak for 5 minutes, then dehydrate and dry.

name: pipe unblocker
ratio: 100g baking soda, 100ml white vinegar, 500ml hot water
steps: First pour baking soda into the clogged pipe, then white vinegar, let it sit for 15 minutes after the reaction produces bubbles, and finally pour hot water to flush the pipe.
safetyNote: A large number of bubbles will be produced during the reaction, so please do not observe it at close range. Do not mix with commercial pipe unblockers.

name: Mildew stain removal detergent
ratio: 100 ml hydrogen peroxide, 20 g baking soda, 200 ml warm water
steps: Dissolve baking soda in warm water, add hydrogen peroxide and mix well. Use a soft brush to scrub the mildew spots, let it sit for 15 minutes, rinse with water, and let it dry.
safetyNote: Hydrogen peroxide has bleaching properties. Please test wash colored fabrics in an inconspicuous area first.

name: Fruit and vegetable cleaning liquid
ratio: 15g baking soda, 10g salt, 1000ml warm water
steps: Dissolve baking soda and salt in warm water and mix well. Soak the fruits and vegetables for 10 minutes, wash them gently, and then rinse them twice with water before eating.

name: Shoe upper stain remover
ratio: 20 grams of toothpaste, 10 grams of baking soda, 50 ml of warm water
steps: Mix toothpaste with baking soda and add warm water to make a paste. Use a soft-bristled brush to gently brush the stains on the shoe surface, then wipe off the residue with a damp cloth and dry in the shade.
safetyNote: For leather and suede materials, please try brushing on an inconspicuous area first to avoid fading or damage.

name: Motor oil hand washing powder
ratio: 100g sawdust, 30g washing powder, 20g soap powder
steps: After the sawdust is dried, mix it thoroughly with washing powder and soap powder and put it into a container. When washing your hands, take an appropriate amount and rub it to remove machine oil and oil stains on your hands, then rinse with water.
safetyNote: The sawdust needs to be dry and mold-free. People with skin allergies should use it with caution.

name: cardigan detergent
ratio: 20 ml baby shampoo, 10 ml white vinegar, 2000 ml cold water
steps: Add baby shampoo and white vinegar to cold water and mix well. Turn the sweater inside out and soak it for 15 minutes. Press gently to wash without rubbing. Rinse with clean water and lay it flat to dry in the shade.
safetyNote: Do not wash with hot water to avoid shrinkage and deformation of the wool.

name: cutting board disinfectant
ratio: 30g salt, 1 lemon, 500ml hot water
steps: The salt is dissolved in hot water and lemon juice is mixed into the solution. Scrub the surface of the cutting board with the solution, let it sit for 10 minutes, then rinse with water and wipe dry to remove odors and minor stains.
safetyNote: Be careful to prevent scalding when operating in hot water. Wooden chopping boards should not be soaked for a long time.

name: baby clothes detergent
ratio: 40g castile soap, 20g baking soda, 1ml lavender essential oil
steps: Dissolve the castile soap shavings in warm water, add baking soda and essential oils and mix well. Bottle cool and use 30 ml at a time for hand washing baby clothes.
safetyNote: The amount of essential oil should be small, and be sure to rinse thoroughly after washing.

name: carpet dry cleaning powder
ratio: 150g cornstarch, 50g baking soda, 3ml essential oil
steps: Mix cornstarch and baking soda, drop in essential oil and mix well. Spread evenly on the carpet surface, let it sit for 30 minutes and then remove it thoroughly with a vacuum cleaner.
safetyNote: Clean the carpet with a vacuum cleaner before use. For dark carpets, please try spreading on an inconspicuous area first.

name: coffee machine descaling fluid
ratio: 25g citric acid, 500ml water
steps: Dissolve citric acid in water and pour it into the water tank. Run the brewing program to let the solution pass through the pipes. Let it sit for 15 minutes and then rinse with clean water 2 to 3 times.
safetyNote: When descaling, follow the equipment instructions to avoid the solution coming into contact with electrical components.

name: leather goods cleaning milk
ratio: 20g saddle soap, 10g lanolin, 5g beeswax
steps: Add saddle soap to water and make a foam, add lanolin and melted beeswax and mix well. Use a soft cloth to gently wipe the leather goods, then polish with a dry cloth.
safetyNote: Before use, test the leather in a hidden area to avoid discoloration.

name: Denim color fixing lotion
ratio: 50 grams of salt, 30 ml of white vinegar, 3000 ml of cold water
steps: Dissolve salt and white vinegar in cold water, turn the jeans inside out and soak them for 30 minutes. Gently rub and rinse with water. Use for the first wash can slow down fading.
safetyNote: Do not use hot water and avoid washing with other clothes to prevent staining.

name: washing machine tank cleaner
ratio: 100g baking soda, 50g citric acid, 500ml warm water
steps: Dissolve baking soda and citric acid in warm water and pour it into the washing machine tub. Run the drum self-cleaning or idle washing program, and rinse it with clean water after completion.
safetyNote: Do not put clothes in when cleaning, as the reaction may produce bubbles.

name: tableware bleaching and disinfecting liquid
ratio: 10g bleaching powder, 1000ml water
steps: Dissolve bleaching powder in water and soak the tableware to be disinfected for 10 minutes. After taking it out, rinse it repeatedly with clean water until there is no odor, dry it before use.
safetyNote: Bleach powder is corrosive, please wear gloves and cannot be used on aluminum or colored tableware.

name: natural lip balm
ratio: Beeswax 10g, coconut oil 20g, vitamin E 2ml
steps: Heat beeswax and coconut oil over water to melt. Remove from heat and add vitamin E and stir evenly. Pour into an empty lipstick tube and let cool to set before use.

name: hand cream
ratio: 30g shea butter, 20g olive oil, 8g beeswax, 2ml essential oil
steps: Heat beeswax over water and melt, add shea butter and olive oil and stir. After cooling, add essential oil, beat until creamy, put into jar and refrigerate.
safetyNote: If you have sensitive skin, please do a skin test first.

name: Oatmeal Honey Mask
ratio: 30g oat flour, 15g honey, 30ml milk
steps: Make a paste of oatmeal powder and milk, add honey and mix well. Apply to face for 15 minutes, rinse with warm water, use 1 to 2 times a week.
safetyNote: Do not use if you are allergic to dairy products or honey.

name: Brown Sugar Body Scrub
ratio: 50g brown sugar, 30ml olive oil, 10ml lemon juice
steps: Mix brown sugar and olive oil, add lemon juice and mix well. Take an appropriate amount and massage the skin when bathing. Rinse with water and the skin will become smooth and moisturized.
safetyNote: Do not use when there is a wound on the skin and avoid contact with eyes.

name: Aloe Vera Moisturizing Gel
ratio: 80 g aloe vera gel, 10 ml glycerin, 1 ml essential oil
steps: Mix aloe vera gel and glycerin until smooth, add essential oil and mix well. Store in an airtight jar and refrigerate, and can be used to moisturize the face or body.

name: Food waste enzyme fertilizer
ratio: 300 grams of peel leaves, 100 grams of brown sugar, 1000 ml of water
steps: Chop the peel, put it into a sealed bottle with brown sugar and water, leaving one third of the space. Open the lid every day to deflate, and dilute 500 times after fermentation for 3 months to water the flowers.
safetyNote: Gas will be produced during fermentation. Do not tighten the bottle cap too tightly and avoid direct sunlight.

name: Bean cake liquid fertilizer
ratio: 100 g bean cake, 1000 ml water
steps: The bean cake is crushed and then soaked in water, sealed and fermented for 15 to 20 days, stirring several times during this period. Take the supernatant and dilute it 10 times to water the plants.
safetyNote: The fermentation liquid has a peculiar smell, so please operate it in a ventilated place and avoid storing it indoors.

name: Plant ash potash fertilizer
ratio: 50 grams of plant ash, 1000 ml of water
steps: Soak the plant ash in water for 24 hours, filter and take the leachate. Dilute it 5 times to water potassium-loving plants to promote flowering and fruiting.
safetyNote: Plant ash is alkaline and cannot be mixed with acidic fertilizers, and the dosage should not be excessive.

name: Egg shell calcium fertilizer
ratio: 50 grams of egg shells, 50 ml of white vinegar, 500 ml of water
steps: Wash the egg shells, dry them, crush them, and add white vinegar to react to create bubbles. After the reaction is completed, add water to dilute it and water the flowers to replenish calcium.
safetyNote: Carbon dioxide bubbles will be produced during the reaction, so please operate in a ventilated area.

name: rice water fertilizer
ratio: 1000 ml rice water, 50 g orange peel
steps: Pour rice water into a bottle and add orange peel pieces. Sealed fermentation for 7 to 10 days, diluted 3 times and then watered to provide nitrogen and phosphorus nutrients.
safetyNote: The fermentation time should not be too long to avoid the production of odor and bacteria.

name: soy wax scented candle
ratio: 200 g soy wax, 5 ml essential oil, 1 candle wick
steps: Heat the soy wax over water to melt, then remove from the heat and add essential oil and stir. The candle wick is fixed in the center of the container, and the wax liquid is poured in to cool and solidify.
safetyNote: Be careful of burns when melting wax liquid, and stay away from flammable materials when igniting.

name: beeswax candle
ratio: 150g beeswax, 30g coconut oil, 1 candle wick
steps: Heat beeswax and coconut oil over water until completely melted, stir evenly. After fixing the candle wick, pour it into the mold, cool it and demould it to become a candle.
safetyNote: When burning, please place it on a fireproof candlestick and keep people away from the fire source.

name: smokeless paraffin candle
ratio: 180g paraffin, 20g stearic acid, 1 candle wick
steps: The paraffin and stearic acid are mixed and heated over water to melt, and then poured into the mold with the candle wick fixed. Once completely cooled, it can be lit and used.
safetyNote: When paraffin is heated, the temperature is relatively high, so be careful to prevent scalding and maintain ventilation.

name: jelly wax candle
ratio: 150 grams of jelly wax, appropriate amount of dye, 3 ml of essential oil
steps: Heat the jelly wax over water to 80°C to melt, add dye and essential oil and stir. Pour into a glass container, embed decorations and let cool to set.
safetyNote: Do not insert ordinary candle wicks into the jelly wax and light it. It is only for viewing or used with LED lights.

name: emergency oil lamp
ratio: 50 ml cooking oil, 1 cotton string, 1 metal cap
steps: The metal cover is perforated and a cotton thread is inserted into it to serve as a wick, and cooking oil is poured in to submerge the lower end of the wick. Light the wick and it can be used as emergency lighting.
safetyNote: For emergency use only. Someone must supervise when burning and keep away from flammable materials.

name: Olive oil handmade soap
ratio: 500 g olive oil, 64 g sodium hydroxide, 160 ml water
steps: Dissolve sodium hydroxide in water and cool down, slowly pour into olive oil and stir until thick. Pour into the mold and keep warm for 24 hours. After unmoulding, let the soap mature for 4 weeks.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive, so you must wear gloves and goggles and operate in a ventilated area.

name: honey milk soap
ratio: 200g coconut oil, 300g olive oil, 71g sodium hydroxide, 20g honey, 50ml milk
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour it into the fat and stir, add honey and milk and continue stirring until thick. Put it into the mold to keep it warm, and let it dry for 4 weeks before use after demolding.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be handled with protective equipment.

name: coffee scrub soap
ratio: 400g coconut oil, 56g sodium hydroxide, 30g coffee grounds, 140ml water
steps: Stir the lye and fat until thick, add the coffee grounds and mix well. Pour into the mold, unmold and let the soap dry for 4 weeks. It has both cleaning and scrubbing effects when used.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive, so please wear gloves and goggles when handling it.

name: Mugwort anti-itch soap
ratio: 350g coconut oil, 50g sodium hydroxide, 100ml mugwort juice, 50ml water
steps: Dissolve sodium hydroxide in mugwort juice and water to cool down, pour in coconut oil and stir until thick. After being placed in the mold and kept warm, the soap is allowed to mature for 4 weeks before being removed from the mold.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be handled in a ventilated area wearing protective equipment.

name: Activated Charcoal Stain Remover Soap
ratio: 400g coconut oil, 56g sodium hydroxide, 15g activated charcoal powder, 140ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the oil and stir, add activated charcoal powder and stir until thick. Pour into molds and let the soap dry for 4 weeks to clean oily hands.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive. Avoid inhaling activated carbon powder. Please wear a mask.

name: Peppermint repellent spray
ratio: Menthol 5g, alcohol 100ml, water 50ml
steps: Dissolve menthol in alcohol, add water, shake well and put into a spray bottle. Spray on the gaps and corners of doors and windows to repel mosquitoes, flies and cockroaches.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, keep away from fire sources. Avoid contact with eyes and children.

name: Citronella Mosquito Repellent
ratio: Lemongrass essential oil 10 ml, carrier oil 30 ml, alcohol 60 ml
steps: Mix lemongrass essential oil with carrier oil, add alcohol and shake well. Apply to exposed skin or spray onto clothing to help repel mosquitoes.
safetyNote: Pregnant women, infants and those with sensitive skin should use with caution and avoid contact with mucous membranes.

name: Zanthoxylum bungeanum insect repellent pack
ratio: 30 grams of Sichuan peppercorns, 20 grams of dried chilies, 10 grams of camphor
steps: Mix the three raw materials evenly, put them into gauze bags and tie them tightly. Placed in the corners of cabinets and wardrobes, it can repel rice bugs and borers.
safetyNote: Camphor is toxic, please keep it out of the reach of children and is not edible.

name: natural mosquito repellent
ratio: 30g pyrethrum powder, 50g sawdust, 20g sticky powder
steps: Mix the raw materials evenly, add an appropriate amount of water and knead into a ball, then press it into a mosquito-repellent incense shape with a mold. Dry in the shade for 5 days and then light before use. Keep the room well ventilated.
safetyNote: People should leave the room when burning, pay attention to fire prevention, and cannot be used in confined spaces.

name: Boric acid cockroach bait
ratio: 20g boric acid, 30g powdered sugar, 20g flour
steps: Mix boric acid, powdered sugar and flour evenly, add a little water and form into small balls. Placed in cockroach-infested corners, it can trap and kill cockroaches.
safetyNote: Boric acid is not edible and must be kept out of the reach of children and pets.

name: wood preservative oil
ratio: Creosote 100 ml, kerosene 50 ml
steps: Mix creosote and kerosene evenly and apply to the dry wood surface with a brush. Exterior wood components slow down decay and insect infestation.
safetyNote: Creosote is toxic and flammable, please wear gloves, operate in a well-ventilated area, and keep away from sources of fire.

name: Kimchi antiseptic brine
ratio: 100 grams of salt, 1000 ml of water, 5 grams of Sichuan peppercorns
steps: Dissolve salt in water, add Sichuan peppercorns, boil and cool. Wash the vegetables, soak them in salt water and seal them for pickling to extend their storage time.
safetyNote: The pickling container must be clean and sealed. Do not eat if there is an odor or mildew.

name: Leather antifungal agent
ratio: Alcohol 80 ml, tea tree essential oil 3 ml, glycerin 10 ml
steps: Mix alcohol and glycerin, add tea tree essential oil and shake well. Use a soft cloth to gently wipe the leather surface to prevent mildew and bacterial growth.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, so test it in a hidden place before use to avoid fading.

name: book insect repellent
ratio: 20 grams of mothballs, 10 grams of bay leaves, appropriate amount of cotton
steps: Mash the mothballs and mix them with fragrant leaves, wrap them in cotton and pack them separately. Place it in bookcases and wardrobes to prevent moths from chewing on books and clothing.
safetyNote: Mothballs are poisonous, please keep them away from children and food, and do not eat them accidentally.

name: Metal anti-rust wax
ratio: 30 grams of paraffin, 40 grams of petroleum jelly, 30 ml of engine oil
steps: Heat the paraffin over water to melt, add Vaseline and engine oil and stir evenly. After cooling, it is applied to the metal surface to isolate air and prevent rust.
safetyNote: Used engine oil may contain harmful substances, so please wear gloves when handling it.

name: Simple fireproof mud
ratio: 200 grams of clay, 50 grams of salt, 80 ml of water
steps: Mix the clay and salt evenly, then gradually add water and knead until it becomes plastic. It can seal pipe holes or temporarily insulate, and its hardness increases after drying.
safetyNote: It is only for temporary protection purposes and cannot replace professional fireproof materials.

name: homemade sparkling water
ratio: 2g baking soda, 2g citric acid, 10g sugar, 300ml water
steps: Dissolve sugar in water, add baking soda and citric acid respectively. Mix before drinking to create bubbles, creating a refreshing sparkling drink.
safetyNote: Ready to mix and drink now. Do not keep it for a long time. Adult supervision is required for children to drink.

name: Handmade clay
ratio: 200g flour, 100g salt, 20ml cooking oil, appropriate amount of food coloring
steps: Mix flour and salt, add oil and coloring, gradually add water and knead until it forms a dough that is not sticky. Sealed and stored, it can be hand-shaped by children.
safetyNote: Although it is a food-grade raw material, it is still not edible and needs to be used under adult supervision.

name: wardrobe desiccant
ratio: 50 grams of quicklime, 30 grams of calcium chloride
steps: Mix quicklime and calcium chloride evenly, put it into a breathable bag and tie it tightly. Hang in humid wardrobes to absorb excess moisture and protect against moisture.
safetyNote: Quicklime releases heat when exposed to water. Keep out of reach of children and avoid getting wet.

name: Winter antifreeze glass water
ratio: 300 ml alcohol, 10 ml dish soap, 200 ml water
steps: Mix alcohol, dish soap and water evenly and put into a spray bottle. Used for car windshield cleaning, it is not easy to freeze at low temperatures.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, keep away from fire sources, and only be used on the exterior glass of the car.

name: Rose Moisturizing Hydrosol
ratio: 50 g rose petals, 500 ml distilled water, 10 ml glycerin
steps: Distill the rose petals and distilled water over low heat or soak them for a long time and then filter. Add the filtrate to glycerin and shake well. Put it in a spray bottle and use it as a facial moisturizer or makeup setting spray.
safetyNote: Freshly prepared products have a short shelf life and are recommended to be refrigerated and used within seven days.

name: Tea tree acne spotting solution
ratio: Tea tree essential oil 3 ml, aloe vera gel 30 g, distilled water 10 ml
steps: After mixing aloe vera gel with distilled water, drop in tea tree essential oil and mix thoroughly. Pour into a roll-on bottle and apply onto acne areas after cleansing, avoiding large areas.
safetyNote: The concentration of essential oil is high, so if you have sensitive skin, please do a skin test first, avoiding the eye area.

name: Coconut Oil Makeup Remover Balm
ratio: Coconut oil 40g, beeswax 8g, vitamin E 2ml
steps: Melt the beeswax over water, add coconut oil and vitamin E and mix well. After cooling slightly, put it into a jar to solidify. Apply a small amount and massage on the face to dissolve the makeup, then emulsify and wash off with warm water.
safetyNote: For oily and acne-prone skin, please control the dosage and cleanse twice after removal.

name: Chamomile Soothing Spray
ratio: 20 g dried chamomile, 200 ml distilled water, 5 ml glycerin
steps: Soak chamomile in hot water for 30 minutes and then filter. Cool the filtrate and add glycerin and mix. Put it in a spray bottle and spray it on your face to soothe it after sun exposure or when you are sensitive.
safetyNote: Do not use if you are allergic to Asteraceae plants.

name: Sea Salt Styling Spray
ratio: 5g sea salt, 200ml warm water, 2ml essential oil
steps: Dissolve sea salt in warm water. Once completely dissolved, add essential oil and shake well. Pour into a spray bottle and spray onto semi-dry hair to create natural texture.
safetyNote: Do not use if there is a wound or inflammation on the scalp.

name: Bamboo Charcoal Cleansing Mask
ratio: 10 grams of bamboo charcoal powder, 20 grams of kaolin clay, 30 ml of water
steps: Bamboo charcoal powder is mixed with kaolin clay and gradually added with water to form a spreadable mud. Apply evenly on face, wait until semi-dry, then rinse with warm water, once or twice a week.
safetyNote: Avoid inhaling the bamboo charcoal powder, and avoid the eyes and lips when applying it on the face.

name: Vitamin E nail polish
ratio: 20 ml olive oil, 5 ml vitamin E, 1 ml lemon essential oil
steps: Mix olive oil and vitamin E, add lemon essential oil and shake well. Use a small brush or cotton swab to apply to nails and nail edges to moisturize and prevent hangnails.
safetyNote: Lemon essential oil is photosensitive, so avoid exposure to strong light after applying it.

name: Mint cooling foot lotion
ratio: 50g aloe vera gel, 2g menthol, 5ml glycerin
steps: Dissolve menthol with a small amount of glycerin, then mix thoroughly with aloe vera gel. Put it in a tube and apply it to your feet after bathing to cool down and relieve fatigue.
safetyNote: Do not use on damaged skin and avoid contact with eyes.

name: coffee eye mask
ratio: 5g coffee powder, 15g yogurt, 5g honey
steps: Mix coffee powder with yogurt and honey into a paste, apply it under the eyes for 10 minutes and then rinse with warm water. Temporarily reduces puffiness and refreshes the eye area.
safetyNote: For external use only, avoid entering eyes. People who are sensitive to caffeine should use with caution.

name: Banana peel potassium fertilizer
ratio: 200 g banana peel, 800 ml water
steps: Cut the banana peels into pieces and soak them in water, seal them and ferment them for 5 to 7 days. During this period, open the lid every day to let the air out. Dilute the leachate 5 times to water potassium-loving plants.
safetyNote: The fermentation liquid has a sour smell. Please operate it in a ventilated place and avoid long-term storage indoors.

name: Tea residue nitrogen fertilizer
ratio: 100 grams of tea leaves, 500 ml of water
steps: Add the tea leaves to water and soak for 24 hours, then filter and take the tea liquid. Dilute it 3 times to water foliage plants to supplement nitrogen and make the leaves dark green.
safetyNote: Unfermented tea residues should not be directly buried thickly on the surface of potting soil to avoid the growth of mold.

name: Watermelon rind liquid fertilizer
ratio: 300g watermelon rind, 50g brown sugar, 800ml water
steps: Chop the watermelon rind into a bottle with brown sugar and water, leaving one third of the space for sealing and fermentation. After two weeks, take the supernatant and dilute it 10 times to water the flowers.
safetyNote: Gas will be produced during fermentation, so the lid needs to be opened regularly to deflate and avoid direct sunlight.

name: Fish scale calcium phosphate fertilizer
ratio: 80 g fish scales, 100 ml white vinegar, 400 ml water
steps: Wash and dry the fish scales, add white vinegar to react until the bubbles subside, then add water to dilute. After filtering, take the clear liquid to water the flowers to supplement calcium and phosphorus.
safetyNote: Gas will be produced during the reaction. Please operate in a ventilated place. Do not pour the waste liquid into the sewer and discharge it in large quantities.

name: Tobacco insect repellent fertilizer
ratio: 20 grams of shredded tobacco, 1000 ml of water
steps: The cut tobacco is soaked in water for 24 hours, filtered and the leachate taken. Dilute it 5 times and spray or irrigate the roots to help control some underground pests.
safetyNote: The leachate is toxic. Please wear gloves when operating and avoid contact with children and pets.

name: Crab shell phosphate fertilizer
ratio: 100 g crab shell, 800 ml water
steps: Wash the crab shells, dry them, grind them, add water to soak them and ferment them for about 20 days, stirring several times during this period. Dilute the supernatant 8 times to water the flowering plants.
safetyNote: The fermentation process has a peculiar smell, so it is recommended to conduct it outdoors or in a ventilated place.

name: Peanut dry cake fertilizer
ratio: 80 grams of peanuts, 1000 ml of water, 30 grams of brown sugar
steps: The peanuts are crushed, mixed with brown sugar and water, and fermented in a sealed container for 15 days. Dilute the fermentation liquid 15 times and water it to provide nitrogen, phosphorus and potassium nutrients.
safetyNote: Concentrated fertilizers cannot be irrigated directly to avoid burning the roots and must be fully diluted.

name: fallen leaf compost liquid
ratio: 500 grams of fallen leaves, 200 grams of food waste, 1500 ml of water
steps: Put the fallen leaves and food waste into the compost bucket in layers, and add water until it is moist but not waterlogged. Turn over once a month, and dilute the decomposition liquid 20 times after 3 months for use.
safetyNote: Unripe compost cannot be used directly to avoid carrying germs and insect eggs.

name: Lemongrass Mosquito Repellent Candle
ratio: 180 g soy wax, 4 ml lemongrass essential oil, 2 ml lemon essential oil, 1 candle wick
steps: Heat the soy wax over water to melt, then remove from the heat and add essential oil and stir evenly. After fixing the candle wick, pour it into the container, cool and solidify, and then use it outdoors for a short period of time.
safetyNote: Keep away from flammable materials and children when igniting, and do not burn in a closed space for a long time.

name: layered colorful candles
ratio: 240 grams of paraffin wax, 3 colors of candle dye, 1 candle wick
steps: The paraffin is melted in three parts and different dyes are added. After the first layer has solidified slightly, pour in the second layer, repeat the operation to create a layered effect, and finally insert the candle wick.
safetyNote: The temperature is high when melting wax, so be careful to prevent scalding and maintain ventilation.

name: palm tealight
ratio: 30 grams of soy wax, 1 candle wick, 1 metal tealight cup
steps: Heat and melt the soy wax, fix the candle wick in the center of the tealight cup, and pour the wax liquid until it is eighty-full. After cooling, it becomes a small tealight that can be replaced repeatedly.
safetyNote: When burning, it must be placed in a fireproof candlestick and people should be kept away from the fire source.

name: Dried flower embedded candle
ratio: 120 grams of jelly wax, appropriate amount of dried flowers, 2 ml of essential oil
steps: Heat the jelly wax to 80°C to melt and add essential oils. First pour a small amount of liquid wax to fix the position of the dried flowers, then fill the container, and then make a decorative candle after cooling.
safetyNote: Dried flower embedded candles may produce black smoke when burning. It is recommended to use them mainly for viewing or to use them with LED lights.

name: coffee scented candle
ratio: 200g soy wax, 10g coffee powder, 1 candle wick
steps: Once the soy wax is melted, remove from heat, add coffee powder and stir gently. After fixing the candle wick, pour it into the mold, cool it and release it to release the coffee aroma.
safetyNote: Coffee powder particles may affect combustion stability. Please supervise the first ignition.

name: Fragrance melt wax block
ratio: 100g soy wax, 20g beeswax, 5ml essential oil
steps: Mix soy wax and beeswax and heat to melt, add essential oil and pour into the silicone grid to cool. When using, place a piece on a melted wax lamp to heat and release the aroma, no candle wick is required.
safetyNote: Do not add water when using the melted wax lamp, and keep away from flammable materials to prevent burns.

name: birthday number candles
ratio: 60 grams of paraffin wax, appropriate amount of candle dye, 1 cotton wick
steps: After the paraffin is melted, the dye is added, poured into the digital mold, and a cotton wick is inserted. Unmold after complete cooling and can be placed on the cake for a short time to light.
safetyNote: Please remove the cake from the cake before lighting it to avoid wax dripping onto the food.

name: Goat Milk Mild Soap
ratio: 350 g olive oil, 150 g coconut oil, 71 g sodium hydroxide, 160 ml goat milk
steps: Slowly dissolve the sodium hydroxide in the chilled goat's milk to cool down, pour into the mixed fat and stir until thick. Place in the mold and keep warm for 24 hours, then dry for 4 weeks after unmoulding.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive, so you must wear gloves and goggles and operate in a ventilated area.

name: Sea Salt Cleansing Soap
ratio: 400g coconut oil, 56g sodium hydroxide, 25g sea salt, 140ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the fat and stir until trace. Add sea salt and stir lightly to retain the graininess. Pour into molds and allow soap to dry for 4 weeks before body cleansing.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive. Avoid sea salt particles entering your eyes. Please wear protective equipment when operating.

name: Tea tree oil control soap
ratio: 250g coconut oil, 250g olive oil, 71g sodium hydroxide, 10ml tea tree essential oil, 160ml water
steps: Stir the lye and oil until thick, add tea tree essential oil and mix well. After being put into the mold and kept warm, it is removed from the mold and allowed to mature for 4 weeks before the soap can be used for oily skin cleansing.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive, and essential oils must be added at the end to avoid prolonged inhalation in a confined space.

name: red clay mineral soap
ratio: 500 g olive oil, 64 g sodium hydroxide, 20 g red clay powder, 160 ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the olive oil and stir, add the red clay powder and stir until thick. Pour into molds and allow soap to dry for 4 weeks to deeply cleanse skin.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive. Avoid inhaling mineral powder. Please wear a mask.

name: Lithospermum Restorative Soap
ratio: 300g olive oil, 200g coconut oil, 71g sodium hydroxide, 50g comfrey infused oil, 160ml water
steps: Stir the lye, fat and comfrey-soaked oil until trace, place into molds and keep warm. Allow the soap to dry for 4 weeks after removal, suitable for dry or slightly damaged skin.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and must be handled in a ventilated area wearing protective equipment.

name: Rosemary Shampoo Soap
ratio: 300g coconut oil, 100g castor oil, 56g sodium hydroxide, 8ml rosemary essential oil, 140ml water
steps: Stir the lye and fat until thick, add rosemary essential oil and stir lightly. Allow the soap to dry for 4 weeks after being put into the mold. It can be used to clean short hair. It needs to be rinsed with apple cider vinegar after washing.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive. Avoid shampoo soap foam entering your eyes. Please wear gloves when operating.

name: beer foaming soap
ratio: 350g olive oil, 150g coconut oil, 71g sodium hydroxide, 160ml beer
steps: Open the lid of the beer one night in advance to degas it, then cool it down by dissolving the alkali in it. Pour the lye solution into the fat and stir until trace, put it into the mold and keep it warm and let the soap dry for 4 weeks.
safetyNote: The reaction between sodium hydroxide and beer will produce a lot of heat and bubbles, so it must be done slowly in a ventilated area and wearing protective equipment.

name: Turmeric Warming Soap
ratio: 400 g coconut oil, 56 g sodium hydroxide, 8 g turmeric powder, 140 ml water
steps: After the lye solution has cooled, pour in the oil and stir, add turmeric powder and mix well. Pour into molds and allow the soap to dry for 4 weeks before use in winter baths.
safetyNote: Sodium hydroxide is highly corrosive and turmeric is easy to stain. Please wear gloves and protect your clothing when operating.

name: Zanthoxylum bungeanum ant repellent powder
ratio: 30g Sichuan peppercorns, 20g baking soda, 50g cornstarch
steps: Dry and crush the Sichuan peppercorns and mix evenly with baking soda and cornstarch. Sprinkle it in the cracks and corners where ants often hang out to interfere with their movement routes.
safetyNote: Avoid inhalation of powder and keep away from food and reach of children.

name: Citronella Mosquito Repellent
ratio: Lemongrass essential oil 8 ml, alcohol 80 ml, water 20 ml
steps: Dissolve the essential oil in alcohol, add water, shake well, and put into a spray bottle. Spray on exposed skin or clothing before outdoor activities to repel mosquitoes for a short time.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable, keep away from fire sources. Infants and children with sensitive skin please do a skin test first.

name: Garlic insect repellent soak
ratio: 50 grams of garlic, 1000 ml of water, 10 ml of soap solution
steps: Mash garlic and soak in water for 24 hours, filter and add soap solution and shake well. Spraying on plant leaves can help control soft-bodied pests such as aphids.
safetyNote: Before spraying, test spray on a small number of leaves to avoid burning the plants if the concentration is too high.

name: Tobacco water insecticide liquid
ratio: 30 grams of tobacco, 1000 ml of water, 5 ml of soap solution
steps: The tobacco is soaked in water for 48 hours, filtered, and mixed with soap. Spray on damaged plants after dilution to control some chewing pests.
safetyNote: Tobacco leachate is toxic, please wear gloves to avoid contaminating edible parts, and keep out of reach of children.

name: Grapefruit peel insect repellent bag
ratio: 100 grams of grapefruit peel, 15 grams of cloves, 1 cotton bag
steps: Cut the grapefruit peel into strips and dry them, mix them with cloves and put them into a cotton bag and tie them tightly. Place it in a rice jar, wardrobe or kitchen corner to help repel insects and prevent moths.
safetyNote: It needs to be replaced regularly to avoid moisture and mold.

name: Styrax wardrobe insect repellent tablets
ratio: 10 grams of styrax, 40 grams of camphor wood chips, 1 non-woven bag
steps: Styrax and camphor wood chips are mixed evenly, put into non-woven bags and tied tightly. Hanging in the wardrobe, it repels insects and emits a woody fragrance.
safetyNote: Keep spices out of the reach of infants and young children to avoid accidental ingestion.

name: Catnip Cockroach Repellent Powder
ratio: 20g catnip, 10g boric acid, 30g flour
steps: Crush catnip, mix it with boric acid and flour, and sprinkle it on cabinet gaps and cockroach-infested areas. It can assist in repelling and interfering with its activities.
safetyNote: Boric acid is toxic and should be kept away from children and pets. Do not spread it near food.

name: Eucalyptus oil anti-mildew spray
ratio: 5 ml of eucalyptus oil, 50 ml of alcohol, 50 ml of water
steps: Dissolve eucalyptus oil in alcohol, add water, shake well, and put into a spray bottle. Spray on wet corners and inside wardrobes to help prevent mildew and insects.
safetyNote: Alcohol is flammable and eucalyptus oil has a strong smell. Keep it ventilated when using it and avoid contact with eyes.

name: Beeswax Wood Care Oil
ratio: 40 g beeswax, 100 ml linseed oil, 20 ml turpentine
steps: Heat beeswax over water and melt, add linseed oil and turpentine and mix well. After cooling, apply it to the wood surface to prevent moisture and cracking and brighten it.
safetyNote: Turpentine is flammable and irritating. Please operate in a well-ventilated area and away from fire sources.

name: Tung oil anti-mildew coating fluid
ratio: 200 ml raw tung oil, 50 ml turpentine
steps: Mix raw tung oil and turpentine evenly and apply it thinly on the surface of wood or bamboo products with a brush. After drying, a protective film is formed to prevent mildew and moisture.
safetyNote: Tung oil and turpentine are both flammable. Keep away from fire sources and maintain ventilation when operating. Used brushes should be disposed of properly.

name: Silicone grain moisture-proof bag
ratio: 100 grams of dry silica gel, 2 breathable bags
steps: Put the dry silica gel granules into breathable cloth bags and tie them tightly. Place it in a camera box, bookcase or shoe box to absorb moisture and prevent moisture. It can be regenerated in the sun after being saturated.
safetyNote: Silica gel particles are not edible, please keep away from infants and young children.

name: Incense moth-proofing block
ratio: 60 grams of sandalwood powder, 40 grams of nanmu powder, 25 ml of water
steps: Mix sandalwood powder and nanmu powder, add water and knead into a ball, then dry in the shade until completely hard. Place in a bookcase or wardrobe to protect against moths and provide fragrance.
safetyNote: For moth-proofing purposes only, not for use as food or toys for children.

name: Lime drying and moisture-proof agent
ratio: 80 grams of quicklime, 40 grams of calcium chloride, 1 breathable jar
steps: Quicklime and calcium chloride are mixed and put into a breathable tank, and the lid is sealed to leave a gap. Placed in a basement or humid storage room, it can absorb ambient moisture.
safetyNote: Quicklime releases heat when it comes in contact with water. Keep out of reach of children and avoid water getting into the container.

name: Beeswax food sealing wax
ratio: 100g beeswax, 30g coconut oil, 20g resin
steps: Melt the beeswax, coconut oil and resin over water and mix well. Brush it on the surface of cheese or semi-hard food while it's hot, and it will form a removable layer of preservation wax after it cools.
safetyNote: Only suitable for direct consumption of food surfaces, make sure all ingredients are food grade before use.

name: Pine wood insect-proof strips
ratio: 200 grams of pine wood, 10 ml of camphor oil
steps: The pine wood blocks are sawn into strips and sanded, dripped or soaked in camphor oil and dried in the shade. Place in a closet, drawer or storage box to repel insects and absorb odors.
safetyNote: Camphor oil is toxic, please keep it away from children and food, and keep the container sealed.

name: homemade paste
ratio: 50g flour, 2g alum, 300ml water
steps: Mix the flour with a small amount of cold water to make a paste. Dissolve alum in the remaining water and heat it to a slight boil. Slowly pour in the batter and stir until it becomes transparent and sticky. After cooling the paper and labels can be attached.
safetyNote: Be careful to prevent scalding when heating. The paste is ready for use. Do not store it for a long time to avoid deterioration.

name: Purple cabbage acid-base indicator
ratio: 100 g purple cabbage, 300 ml distilled water
steps: Chop purple cabbage and soak or lightly boil it in distilled water, then filter to get the purple filtrate. It turns red when exposed to acid and green when exposed to alkaline, and can be used for simple chemical experiments.
safetyNote: For experimental observation only, not for drinking. It is difficult to clean clothes after dyeing.

name: natural stain remover powder
ratio: 100g baking soda, 50g salt, 20g lemon peel powder
steps: Mix the baking soda, salt and lemon zest powder thoroughly and place in a dusting bottle. After getting wet, sprinkle it on the sink and stove, and scrub with a damp cloth to remove light grease.
safetyNote: It cannot be used on surfaces easily corroded by alkali such as marble and aluminum products.

name: Simple concrete repair mud
ratio: 100 grams of cement, 200 grams of river sand, 60 ml of water
steps: Dry mix the cement and river sand evenly, then gradually add water and stir until it becomes plastic. Fill small holes in the wall or cracks in the floor, smooth them out and then cure them for 24 hours.
safetyNote: Cement is alkaline, so please wear gloves and a mask when operating to avoid inhaling dust.

name: emergency insulation mud
ratio: 300 grams of clay, 50 grams of straw, 100 ml of water
steps: Mix the clay with chopped straw, add water and knead until it becomes plastic. Wrapping it on the surface of outdoor water pipes or temporary facilities can slow down freezing for a short time.
safetyNote: For temporary emergencies only, it is fragile after drying and needs to be inspected and replaced regularly.

name: natural plasticine
ratio: 200g flour, 10g cream of tartar, 20ml cooking oil, appropriate amount of food coloring
steps: Mix flour and cream of tartar, add oil and coloring, gradually heat water and knead until no longer sticky. After cooling, it can be shaped by children, sealed and refrigerated.
safetyNote: Although it is a food-grade raw material, it is still not edible and needs to be used under adult supervision.

name: Simple knife sharpening paste
ratio: 20 grams of green chromium oxide powder, 30 ml of glycerin, 10 ml of water
steps: Chromium oxide powder is mixed with glycerin and water into a paste and applied to leather or wooden slats. The blade is pushed and pulled on the paste surface at a suitable angle to finely polish the blade.
safetyNote: Avoid inhaling the grinding powder. Please wear a mask when operating and be careful of scratches by the blade.

name: Pipe unblocking auxiliary powder
ratio: 100g baking soda, 50g citric acid, 30g salt
steps: Mix the three powders evenly and put them into sealed jars. When unblocking, pour 50 grams into the blocked pipe, add 500 ml of water, let it sit for 30 minutes and then flush.
safetyNote: A large number of bubbles will be produced during the reaction. Do not mix it with chemicals such as bleach to avoid splashing into the eyes.

name: Osmanthus infused sesame oil
ratio: 50g fresh osmanthus, 200ml olive oil, 20ml alcohol
steps: The osmanthus and olive oil are sealed and soaked for 7 days, and shaken daily. After filtering, add a small amount of alcohol to preserve it and can be used to add fragrance to your wrists or hair.
safetyNote: Freshly made infused oils are prone to oxidation, so it is recommended to be refrigerated and used within two weeks. For sensitive skin, please do a skin test first.

name: agarwood incense stick
ratio: 40 grams of agarwood powder, 60 grams of nanmu powder, 35 ml of water
steps: Mix agarwood powder and nanmu powder, gradually add water and knead until it becomes plastic, and then extrude it with an incense mold. After drying in the shade for 5 days, you can light the incense in a ventilated place.
safetyNote: When burning, please use fire-proof incense sticks, keep the room ventilated, and keep people away from the fire source.

name: Jasmine fragrance wax tablets
ratio: 30g beeswax, 3ml jasmine essential oil, 2 pieces of non-woven fabric
steps: When the beeswax melts, remove from the heat and add jasmine essential oil. Dip the non-woven fabric into the wax liquid, take it out and cool it, then make fragrant wax pieces and hang them in the wardrobe to slowly release the fragrance.
safetyNote: The wax chips cannot be ignited and are only for hanging to release fragrance and avoid high temperature environments.

name: Citrus peel air freshener
ratio: 100 g orange peel, 5 g cinnamon, 5 g cloves, 500 ml water
steps: Add orange peel and spices to water and simmer over low heat for 30 minutes. After the filtrate cools, put it into a spray bottle. Spray indoors for a refreshing citrus scent.
safetyNote: Freshly prepared liquids are prone to spoilage, so it is recommended to be refrigerated and used within three days.

name: Sleep Lavender Pillow Bag
ratio: 40g dried lavender, 20g dried chamomile, 1 cotton bag
steps: Mix lavender and chamomile evenly, put into cotton bag and tie tightly. Place it next to your pillow to release the herbal aroma that helps you sleep at night.
safetyNote: Do not use if you are allergic to pollen or Asteraceae plants.

name: electric kettle descaling tablets
ratio: 40g citric acid, 20g baking soda
steps: Mix citric acid with baking soda and press into tablets or keep in powder form. Add 15 grams at a time to the kettle, add water to boil, soak for 30 minutes, rinse to remove scale.
safetyNote: Bubbles will be generated during the reaction, so the lid must not be completely sealed and cannot be used in aluminum containers.

name: leather care cleaner
ratio: 50 ml olive oil, 20 ml white vinegar, 2 ml essential oil
steps: Mix olive oil, white vinegar and essential oil, shake well, and apply a small amount to a soft cloth. Gently wipe the leather surface to remove dirt and maintain it, then polish with a dry cloth.
safetyNote: Before use, test it in a hidden area to avoid fading or leaving oil stains.

name: keyboard cleaning gel
ratio: 100 ml glue, 5 g borax, 50 ml water
